figures.caption: Figure 6: The set of feasible (W,IC) and the optimal welfare W ∗ for f (θ) = 8θ(1−θ)− 1.
Figure 4: Under a generic projection of a polytope to an n-dimensional space, any point in the relative interior of an n-dimensional face is projected to the interior of the image.
Figure 5: Payoff from action 1 as a function of the fraction of agents playing this action. Highlighted zeros correspond to stable Nash equilibria, others to unstable.
Figure 1: A non-degenerate objective (left panel) and a degenerate one (right panel).

Figure 3: Projections of a cube onto two planes: A projection along the cube’s edge collapses pairs of vertices, so each corner of the resulting square traces back to the whole edge. By contrast, a generic projection forms a hexagon whose corners trace back to a single vertex of the cube.
